injectFailed: Message couldn't be injected — press Enter to send as a new turn.
switchModeDescription: This will start a new conversation. Your current conversation is saved and accessible from the sidebar.
placeholderInterrupt: Send a message to interrupt the agent...
placeholderReact: Ask the agent to solve a problem... (Type / for commands, drag files to attach)
placeholderDag: Describe a multi-step task for the DAG planner... (Type / for commands, drag files to attach)
placeholderAuto: Ask anything — the best mode will be chosen automatically... (Type / for commands, drag files to attach)
modeStandardTooltip: Standard (ReAct) — Quick response with flexible tool use
modePlannerTooltip: Planner (DAG) — Breaks down tasks into a structured plan
modeAutoTooltip: Auto — Automatically choose Standard (ReAct) or Planner (DAG) based on query complexity
autoAgentDescription: Automatically delegates to the best agent
compactDivider: Earlier context ({count} messages) was summarized by AI
autoSubtitle: Automatically picks the best execution strategy
evidenceInsufficientDetail: Confidence {confidence}% is below the required threshold of {threshold}%.
evidenceInsufficientHint: The retrieved evidence is not reliable enough to provide a confident answer.
exportDescription: Choose a format and detail level for the export.
exportFormatMdDesc: Formatted text with headers and code blocks
exportFormatPdfDesc: Portable document format with formatted text
exportDetailsDescription: Include tool calls, reasoning steps, and execution stats

slash.modeDesc: Switch between Auto, Standard, and Planner mode

react: Find the top 5 most-starred repositories on GitHub -- which one is #1 and how many stars does it have?
Look up 'Sorting algorithm' on Wikipedia and create a comparison table of Bubble Sort, Quick Sort, and Merge Sort by time complexity
Simulate the Monty Hall problem 10,000 times -- should you switch doors? Show the win rates
Write Python to calculate the first 30 Fibonacci numbers and their golden ratio convergence, save the results as fibonacci.csv
What time is it in New York, London, and Tokyo right now? Use template_render to create a styled HTML world clock dashboard
Find a public REST API with dummy posts, fetch 100 of them, then write Python to rank the top 5 most prolific authors and their average post title length
dag: Look up the top 3 most-starred repos for Facebook, Google, and Microsoft on GitHub in parallel, then compare which tech giant has the highest average stars
Look up Python, Rust, and Go on Wikipedia in parallel, then build a comparison table: creation year, creator, type system, and key strength
Generate a 20x20 maze, then race BFS vs DFS in parallel -- which explores fewer cells? Visualize both solutions in ASCII art
Compute the first 50 Fibonacci numbers, first 50 primes, and first 50 triangular numbers in parallel, then find which numbers appear in multiple sequences
Get the current time in New York, London, Tokyo, Sydney, and Dubai in parallel, then use template_render to create a styled HTML world clock dashboard
Find a public REST API with posts and todos, fetch both in parallel, then write Python to correlate: do the most prolific posters also complete the most todos?
auto: Fetch the Node.js LTS changelog and summarize the 5 most impactful changes for a typical backend developer
Simulate rolling two dice 50,000 times — print a frequency table of every possible sum and identify the most and least common outcomes
Fetch 100 posts from JSONPlaceholder, rank the top 5 most active users by post count, and use template_render to display their stats as a styled leaderboard card
Look up Claude Opus 4 and GPT-5 on Wikipedia — compare their release dates, context windows, and flagship capabilities — then produce a clean markdown comparison table
Research React, Vue, and Svelte simultaneously — for each fetch npm weekly downloads, GitHub stars, and latest version — then rank them and produce a styled HTML scorecard with medal badges

In parallel: (1) implement merge sort and quick sort, benchmark both on 10,000 random integers; (2) find all Fibonacci numbers up to index 50 that are also prime; (3) solve N-Queens for N=8 and count all solutions — compile everything into one styled HTML report
